body:
# Feature request

## Summary

After trying out the [Menubar](https://base-ui.com/react/components/menubar) component for the first time, I absolutely fell in love with the composition model and how I can drop my existing (styled) Menu component and use it for a different "modality". It's night and day difference compared to the old world and radix-ui APIs.

Given that in a lot of modern software, a dropdown menu and context menu look visually the same and they only differ in the trigger mechanism, has anyone around here considered to allow using a (styled) Menu as the base for a Context Menu?

## Examples in other libraries

<!-- Provide a link to examples of this component or feature implemented in other libraries (if applicable).  -->

Not another library per se, but I wanted to showcase Linear's Menus and how they are basically the same between context and dropdown. Same goes for other productivity apps like Notion, and bigger players like macOS too!

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/4bd7b51e-a12e-4e88-b3e7-6356437d02d7

## Motivation

<!-- What are you trying to accomplish? Which kind of user is this for? Providing context helps us come up with a solution that is more useful in the real world -->

I feel that having a good solution for menu interop and composition would go a long way into developer ergonomics.

comments.body:
> a dropdown menu and context menu look visually the same and they only differ in the trigger mechanism, has anyone around here considered to allow using a (styled) Menu as the base for a Context Menu

So your aim is to share styles between Menu and ContextMenu?

> > a dropdown menu and context menu look visually the same and they only differ in the trigger mechanism, has anyone around here considered to allow using a (styled) Menu as the base for a Context Menu > > So your aim is to share styles between Menu and ContextMenu? Yeah, but I feel that it goes deeper than that. One of the core tenants of Base UI is composition, and you can truly feel this when you're using `Menu`s inside a `Menubar`. Sure, it would be nice to share styling between the `Menu`/`ContextMenu` and you could even make the argument that that's the job of a higher order theming library. That said, I would rather focus more on how people use software today and then work backwards on how we should design the lego blocks and DX around them. If we look at modern desktop software, there's various apps that embrace (1) using the same styling between `Menu` and `ContextMenu`, and more importantly (2) allow to trigger the same set of actions either through a `Menu` or `ContextMenu`. There's also been a bit of discourse online about this UX pattern, some sparked by [Raphael Schaad](https://x.com/raphaelschaad/status/1884715535063003546). | App | Menu | ContextMenu | |--------------|-----|---------------| | Apple Notes | | | | Notion | | | | Linear | | | Now, if we try to rebuild the feature from above using the latest base ui API, it's almost guaranteed to lead to some awkwardness and the need of a higher level abstraction. Back at @campsite, we ended up building our own DSL (see [Menu/types](https://github.com/campsite/campsite/blob/main/packages/ui/src/Menu/types.ts)), that would allow us to dynamically render actions either as a `DropdownMenu` or `ContextMenu` (see [NotificationOverflowMenu](https://github.com/campsite/campsite/blob/cc14551df7264c4adfdf6d0f6a549c713e0da7a8/apps/web/components/InboxItems/NotificationOverflowMenu.tsx#L13)). Personally, I feel that the base ui `Menu` component should be isomorphic in nature and easily composable with your desired context like `Menubar` and `ContextMenu`.

> (1) using the same styling between Menu and ContextMenu, and more importantly (2) allow to trigger the same set of actions either through a Menu or ContextMenu

Except for `Root` and `Trigger` which are unique, all the context menu parts are direct reexports of regular menu parts so they are interchangeable and you can kind of achieve this without getting too messy: https://stackblitz.com/edit/gyku757p?file=src%2FApp.tsx

I think the bottleneck is it's not possible to "stack" a context menu trigger and a real menu trigger, so even if the API was like Menubar (`<ContextMenu />` without `Root`, maybe `<Menu.ContextMenuTrigger />`), things will start to break when stacking 2 "real" triggers

labels.name: type: enhancement
labels.description: It’s an improvement, but we can’t make up our mind whether it's a bug fix or a new feature.

labels.name: component: context menu
labels.description: Changes related to the context menu component.

title: [context menu] composition with menu
